hero.subheadingText: Sneha teaches classical drawing techniques, from direct charcoal portraiture to complex architectural line perspective, guiding students from tentative markings to confident, beautiful fine artistry.

about.title: About Sneha Bansal
paragraphs: Hello! I am Sneha, a traditional artist and visual discipline mentor based in India. Art is not a collection of happy accidents or unexplainable magic. It is a language of visual relationships, structural perspective, and careful tonal gradation.

My teaching philosophy centers on systematic academic instruction. Whether you are an absolute beginner struggling to draw eyes that line up, or an experienced hobbyist wanting to elevate your portraits, I dismantle the visual world into clear, repeatable mathematical steps.

By guiding you through sight-size triangulation, edge hierarchy refinement, and charcoal value mapping, we will rewrite the software of how you see. Let's train your eye to see truly and your pencil to express with absolute precision.
about.quote: We do not learn to draw to replicate a photo. We learn to draw so we can translate how light rolls over a surface, and make a flat piece of paper breathe.
about.authorRole: Academic Director, Kailaras, Morena, Madhya Pradesh, India

contact.title: Connect with Sneha Bansal
contact.description: Have questions about group programs, specialized portfolio reviews, or personalized coaching? Fill out the portfolio inquiry form below and elevate your sketching foundations.
contact.address: Studio 12, Main Road, Kailaras, Morena, Madhya Pradesh, 476224, India
contact.infoCardText: Based out of Kailaras, Morena, Sneha offers structural art training worldwide. Whether you prefer traditional paper workshops or live interactive screens, get structured answers.
contact.infoCardQuote: Art is not just a collection of drawings. It is a systematic, lovely discipline of learning to see depth, space, and accurate value hierarchies.

gallery.title: Philosopher's Wisdom
gallery.medium: Compressed charcoal, raw vine willow, and white chalk on heavy Canson paper
gallery.description: A study in high chiaroscuro and skin textures. Drawn from a live model session in Siena, Italy. This drawing demonstrates how hard edge-lines can be dissolved entirely into shadow gradients to establish monumental physical volume.

gallery.title: Cathedral of San Frediano
gallery.medium: Iron gall ink, bamboo stylus, and dry-brush sepia washes on vintage paper
gallery.description: An architectural perspective study illustrating 3-point vanishing alignments. This piece showcases controlled hatching density to suggest ancient sun-baked stone and deep, dusty archway negative spaces.

gallery.title: The Florentine Hand Study
gallery.medium: Willow charcoal rods and graphite 4B accents on cream drawing rag
gallery.description: Anatomical study highlighting active hand tendons and structural joint foreshortening. A major focus here is the delicate gradation of light bouncing off the palm versus the dark backdrop shadows.

gallery.title: Study of Roman Torso
gallery.medium: Graphite scale pencils (H, F, HB, 2B, 6B) on hot-pressed Bristol paper
gallery.description: This museum classical plaster cast study teaches students how to render complex spherical surfaces and clean cast-shadow bounds without relying on smudging tools. Made entirely with precise cross-hatching.

gallery.title: Grizzled Forest Pines
gallery.medium: Soft dry earth pastels and charcoal willow lines on Canson Mi-Teintes sand board
gallery.description: Landscape texture exercise exploring contrast between rough pine bark and distant, blurry horizon atmospheres. Soft pastel layers were massed in first and then defined with raw charcoal willow strokes.

gallery.title: The Eye of Leonardo
gallery.medium: Traditional carbon charcoal and standard mechanical pencils on cotton rag
gallery.description: An high-fidelity microscopic study replica of the human eye. Explores the circular muscular structure of the iris and the mirror-like specular highlights that make eyes look liquid and three-dimensional.

studentShowcase.title: Bargue Profile of Dante
studentShowcase.medium: Academic Graphite (HB, 2B, 4B) on Strathmore heavy paper
studentShowcase.description: A dedicated study in line-weight control and outline exactness, practicing Charles Bargue's Sight-Size classical methodology of geometric simplification.
studentShowcase.teacherMentorshipNotes: Clarissa started with zero formal drawing experience. By teaching her to squint and simplify complex contours into straight, angled vectors first, she overcame the fear of free-hand portraiture. Her precision in the forehead slope and nose contour is exceptional.

studentShowcase.title: Michelangelo's David Eye Plaster
studentShowcase.medium: Compressed Charcoal, Willow Sticks, and Kneaded Eraser on Canson Rag
studentShowcase.description: Detailed classical cast study analyzing the dramatic three-dimensional light wrapping around the eye socket, eyelid fold, and brow structure.
studentShowcase.teacherMentorshipNotes: An incredible study in light mechanics. Julian's rendering of the iris hollow and the cast-shadow from the brow projection is perfectly aligned with real light rays. The intermediate stage focuses heavily on translating spheres and cylinders.

studentShowcase.title: Anatomical Grip & Foreshortened Wrist
studentShowcase.medium: Stumped Charcoal, Carbon Dust, and Raw Graphite 6B highlights on Tinted Ingres Paper
studentShowcase.description: An advanced inquiry of active hand bone landmarks, tendon strains, and complex overlapping fingers receding in deeply foreshortened space.
studentShowcase.teacherMentorshipNotes: Hands are notoriously difficult because our minds tend to draw symbols instead of actual shapes. Evelyn analyzed the skeletal structure underneath first. Note how her highlights follow the tendon lines, giving the drawing vibrant muscular realism.

lessons.title: Foundations of Sight
lessons.description: Rebuild your visual system. Learn to bypass mental assumptions and sketch precisely what is in front of you, with secure contours and basic structural value blocks.
curriculum: The 9-step gray scale and charcoal value blocks

lessons.title: Classical Portraiture Mastery
lessons.description: Anatomical analysis of the human facial architecture. Learn how to map muscular forms, direct strong lighting (chiaroscuro), and shade core values smoothly.
curriculum: The Loomis method & structural head planes
Eye, nose, lip, and ear anatomical sub-structures
Establishing core and cast shadow borders
Edge hierarchy (Hard, Firm, Soft, and Lost boundaries)
Subtle value gradient transitions using 6B and woodless graphite

lessons.title: Architectural & Masterwork Perspective
lessons.description: Explore grand perspectives and natural landscapes. Work with classical iron gall sepia ink and bamboo reeds, learning how to combine architectural precision with organic texture.
curriculum: Draped linen study and complex texture rendering
Landscape massing using soft charcoal and ink washes
Analyzing and copying classical master drawings (Da Vinci, Michelangelo)

testimonials.reviewText: Before working with Sneha, my drawings felt like flat cartoon outlines. She completely rewired how I analyze lighting. By understanding where the terminator shadows meet bouncing ambient light, I can finally draw realistic, three-dimensional faces that look alive. Her program is legendary!

I used to struggle intensely with structural perspective. Alignment lines never seemed to match. Sneha taught me classical academic triangulation tricks and 2-point horizon guides. My sketchbooks are now filled with beautiful, structured heritage landmark drawings. Absolutely flawless tutoring.

I thought realism required an innate genetic talent. Sneha showed me that drawing is simply 95% active, focused seeing. Learning to isolate shapes using negative space and values removed my creative blocks entirely. Highly encourage this for anyone starting out with pencil drawing.

achievements.title: Honors & Milestones
paragraphs: Sneha's dedication to classical fine art has been recognized by national institutes and international academy circles. Her precise technical command over charcoal drawing, structural values, and Sight-Size execution has earned her academic distinctions.

Most notably, she has been certified in the elite Drawing Mastery Program by Reyanssh Rahul Academy, establishing her among India's most recommended traditional fine art tutors and drawing authorities.
Under her leadership, her students have gone on to win prestigious youth art prizes and showcase works in national galleries across Madhya Pradesh and beyond.
achievements.metricLeftBackText: Formally accredited by Reyanssh Rahul Academy. Validates professional instruction standard and mastery of graphite, charcoal, and dry media.
achievements.metricRightBackText: Highly recommended fine art authority with certified national-level master classes and standard-defining traditional techniques.
achievements.summaryText: This achievement represents verified completion of rigorous training or recognized academic accomplishments within traditional drawing disciplines. Under her administration, Sneha propagates these deep values to empower her students at the Kalakar Sneha platform.

cards.title: Drawing Mastery Certification
cards.description: Awarded for exceptional mastery of traditional fine art drawing principles, advanced value rendering, and consistent excellence in Sight-Size portrait methods.

cards.title: Outstanding Fine Art Tutor
cards.description: Recognized for her contribution to reviving traditional chiaroscuro academic techniques and mentoring over 450+ students with systematic instructions.

cards.title: Classical Plaster Study First Prize
cards.description: Awarded first place for her detailed graphite plaster cast study replicas demonstrating precise edge-hierarchies and monumental negative volumes.
